# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Deployment for our Node.js microservice. Focus on getting it deployed quickly - we'll optimize resource allocation later based on actual usage metrics. The service handles API requests and connects to a MongoDB database.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: nodejs-api-deployment
  labels:
    app: nodejs-api
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: nodejs-api
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: nodejs-api
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: nodejs-api
          image: your-registry/your-nodejs-app:latest # Replace with your actual image
          ports:
            - containerPort: 3000 # Adjust if your Node.js app listens on a different port
          env:
            - name: MONGO_URI
              value: "mongodb://mongodb-service:27017/mydatabase" # Replace with your MongoDB connection string or a Kubernetes Service name
            - name: NODE_ENV
              value: "production"
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /health # Replace with your application's health check endpoint
              port: 3000
            initialDelaySeconds: 15
            periodSeconds: 20
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /health # Replace with your application's health check endpoint
              port: 3000
            initialDelaySeconds: 5
            periodSeconds: 10
          # No resource requests/limits as per requirement for quick deployment
          # resources:
          #   requests:
          #     cpu: "100m"
          #     memory: "128Mi"
          #   limits:
          #     cpu: "500m"
          #     memory: "512Mi"
